title: Vaccinium oliganthum
taxonomy: Vaccinium oliganthum is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the western United States. This plant is part of the Vaccinium genus, which includes various species of blueberries and cranberries. The classification of Vaccinium oliganthum is further divided into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiospermophyta), class (Magnoliopsida), and order (Ericales). Vaccinium oliganthum is also known by its synonym, Vaccinium scoparium.

intro: Vaccinium oliganthum, also known as the small-fruited blueberry, is a rare and endangered species of shrub in the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant is rich in antioxidants, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections.
